steps.title: Identify the modifier position
steps.content: The main verb “helped” appears at the end of the sentence. A comma after the blank signals that a modifying phrase comes between the noun “authors” and this main verb. The structure [noun], [modifier], [main verb] requires the blank to start a modifier, not a second verb.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content: A participle (the -ing or -ed form of a verb functioning as an adjective) correctly modifies a noun in this position. Option B's “asserting” is the -ing form that heads the phrase “asserting that American publishers' appropriation of their work caused injuries”: “This cadre of renowned authors, asserting that..., helped sow the seeds.” Options A (asserted), C (had asserted), and D (were asserting) are all conjugated verb forms that would function as competing verbs and break the sentence structure. That matches choice B — B is correct.
